title: Peace Talk met Arjan Erkel: vrijheid in een hok onder de grond.
summary: Wat als je alles verliest: je vrijheid, je veiligheid, je vertrouwen in de wereld, en tГіch iets vindt dat niemand je kan afpakken? In deze uitzonderlijke Peace Talk spreekt Arjan Erkel over zijn 607 dagen durende ontvoering in Dagestan. Hij neemt je mee naar een zelfgegraven hok onder de grond, waar hij leerde omgaan met totale machteloosheid, vernedering en angst. Maar ook: waar hij ontdekte dat vrijheid een innerlijke ervaring kan zijn. Met prachtige inzichten, rauwe anekdotes en onverwachte humor vertelt Arjan hoe hij overleefde, wat hij losliet, en wat hij sindsdien nooit meer opgeeft. Een verhaal over mentale kracht en radicale aanvaarding en hoe zelfs de donkerste periodes licht kunnen brengen, voor iedereen die weleens vastzit in het leven. Mis deze niet! title: Annalie Howling | How To Recover Quicker From LifeвҖҷs Setbacks
summary: Setbacks are an inevitable part of life, so what tools are most effective in getting us back on our feet and, perhaps, even stronger than we were before? Annalie Howling is a performance coach who works with leaders in business, elite sport and the Armed Forces, and who has personal experience in recovering from lifeвҖҷs challenges. She explains how a back to basics approach can help, the role of radical self-belief, why helping others can transform your own problems and the importance of finding a renewed purpose in life. In this episode, we have an honest and important conversation about mental health. If you or someone you know is struggling, you are not aloneвҖ”support is available; you can contact the Samaritans at: https://www.samaritans.org/ Annalie HowlingвҖҷs new book вҖҳUnapologetic: Shackle Your Shame, Reclaim Your PowerвҖҷ is out now. title: Working with our minds
summary: Mindfulness meditation, which involves becoming aware of the breath in the present moment, has been a core part of Eastern contemplative practices for thousands of years. Over recent decades however, itвҖҷs вҖҳexplodedвҖҷ throughout the West as scientists have sought to prove the physical and mental benefits of regular practice - like feeling calmer, less stressed and feeling better able to manage emotions.  This week we look at some of the more surprising places where these simple techniques are having a big impact. In Kenya, we learn about the вҖҳmindfulness revolutionвҖҷ that took place in a menвҖҷs high security prison outside Nairobi after a group of inmates and guards were taught mindfulness techniques in 2015. They soon started teaching each other and ten years later itвҖҷs spread to prisons throughout the country and beyond. And we visit Baltimore in the US, where a non-profit organisation has been teaching mindfulness and yoga in inner city schools for over twenty years, giving children growing up with violence and deprivation the tools to manage their emotions and heal trauma. People Fixing The World from the BBC is about brilliant solutions to the world's problems. We'd love you to let us know what you think and to hear about your own solutions. title: Miriam Margolyes on Ageing, Onions, and Matters of the Heart
summary: BAFTA-winning actress Miriam Margolyes joins Dr Oscar Duke for the first appointment of the Bedside Manners podcast. Describing a childhood growing up as the daughter of a GP, Miriam shares how passing wind and earwax were common dinner-time topics. But as someone who has reached a considerable level of fame and following in later life, staying fit and healthy has taken on a new meaningвҖ”as she describes enduring knee replacement surgery and the TAVI (Transcatheter Aortic Valve Implantation) procedure on her heart. She also shares thoughts on the importance of maintaining social connections as you get older and "forcing your mouth to go up at the sides". Plus, Oscar reveals a potential diagnosis relating to the gastric effect of onions. Professor Michael Fertleman from HCA Healthcare UK (a specialist in geriatric medicine and orthopaedic rehabilitation) also joins Oscar to discuss the intricacies of elderly care, the risks and benefits of surgeries for older adults, and the future of care enhanced by technology. This episode is sponsored by HCA Healthcare UK.

title: How Do We Manage... Staying True To Our Values?
summary: In this episode of How Do We Manage Stephanie is joined in the studio by a global fashion icon.В Elizabeth von der Goltz has been a leading name in the fashion industry for decades. She worked her way up from a temp job at Barneys in New York to take on leadership roles at Bergdorf Goodman and Net-a-Porter and later became Chief Merchandising Officer for Matches.com and CEO of Browns. SheвҖҷs now working as a Luxury Fashion & E-Commerce Executive, Board Leader & Strategic Advisor. В  Elizabeth sits down with Stephanie to share her story of drive and self-belief, how she refused to participate in the more toxic elements of the industry, her own вҖңDevil Wears PradaвҖқ moment, and how it feels when things donвҖҷt go to plan at the top of your game. SheвҖҷs also got great advice for anyone just starting their career вҖ“ plus tips on how to stay true to yourself in both your approach to your work, and your own personal style. podcast_name: SLEEP QUICKLY рҹҳҙ - Fall Asleep with Sound
summary: Immerse yourself in the magic of a nighttime forest where soft rain creates a natural symphony of calm. The gentle patter of raindrops on leaves and the damp ground invites a soothing sensory journey, perfect for unwinding and falling asleep. Every drop of this restorative rain wraps your mind in peace, preparing you for a night of deep and restful sleep. This series celebrates the power of natural sounds to calm the mind and encourage peaceful rest. ABOUT THIS CONVERSATION:  In this episode, I speak with Cheryl, who brings insider experience from the pharmaceutical world and a deeply analytical mind to everything thatвҖҷs unfolded over the past few years. We dive into the role of the MHRA, the UKвҖҷs drug regulator, and explore whether itвҖҷs truly serving public health or merely rubber-stamping products handed to it by pharmaceutical companies. Cheryl shares her concerns about the lack of transparency, the failures of pharmacovigilance, and how freedom of information requests have revealed troubling gaps in regulatory oversight, especially in relation to the COVID-19 vaccine rollout. summary: Can a common kitchen herb help stop your next migraine attack before it starts? In this episode of The Migraine Heroes Podcast, host Diane Ducarme reveals a surprising, all-natural migraine ally thatвҖҷs likely sitting in your fridge right now. This humble green herb has been quietly doing the heavy lifting inside the Migraine Heroes AppвҖ”but today, sheвҖҷs finally unveiling its name publicly. YouвҖҷll learn: - How this herb supports your gut, liver, kidneys, and even your brain - The Western science behind its anti-inflammatory, detoxifying, and migraine-calming powers - How Traditional Chinese Medicine uses it to treat specific migraine patterns (like PMS migraines and brain fog) - Who should not use itвҖ”and why Diane blends modern neuroscience with Eastern wisdom to break down exactly how this herb works inside your bodyвҖ”and how to start using it daily for migraine relief. Tune in every Monday and WednesdayвҖ”and start tapping into the medicine nature has already planted in your kitchen. рҹ”— Download the Migraine Heroes App: вҒ вҒ вҒ  рҹ“Ң вҒ App StoreвҒ  рҹ“Ң вҒ Google PlaystoreвҒ  рҹ”— Discover Your Unique Migraine Profile:вҒ вҒ вҒ вҒ  Take our Migraine Diagnostic TestвҒ вҒ вҒ вҒ  to uncover insights about your migraine triggers and solutions. summary: If youвҖҷve ever felt stuck in the cycle of вҖңclean eatingвҖқ one day and bingeing the next, this video is for you. In this episode, Mandy breaks down why labeling food as вҖңgoodвҖқ or вҖңbadвҖқ is one of the biggest drivers of food guilt and emotional stress around eating. We'll explore how the concept of good food choices vs bad foods and the constant good vs bad food narrative damages your relationship with food, leading to restriction, guilt, and shame. Mandy shares how to build a more healthy relationship with food by shifting your mindset, removing the fear, and finding real food freedom. From the psychology behind food labeling, to managing emotional eating, weвҖҷll help you understand how to stop food guilt and give you tools for how to deal with food guilt in your everyday life. If youвҖҷve ever wished you could stop feeling guilty about food, or just eat without overthinking every bite, youвҖҷre not alone. This is your starting point toward a no guilt food mindset and a powerful nutrition mindset shift that actually lasts. title: Unmasking Perfectionism & Impostor Syndrome
summary: In this heartfelt and honest episode, Giana opens up about her long-time relationship with perfectionismвҖ”how it quietly shaped her path, fueled impostor syndrome, and kept her playing small. We explore the sneaky ways perfectionism shows up in teaching yoga, and especially on social media, where carefully curated feeds can end up hiding who we really are. We talk about how striving to appear flawless can hold us back from taking risks, speaking our truth, and feeling like we truly belong in the spaces we've worked so hard to enter. That lingering sense of вҖңnever being enoughвҖқ? Yep, we go there. And we talk about how embracing your full, messy, brilliant self can change everything. Authenticity creates connection. Vulnerability opens doors. This episode is your reminder that your presence doesnвҖҷt have to be perfect to be powerful. Bring the mess. Bring the magic. ThereвҖҷs space for you, just as you are. title: Daughter of an Addict: AshleeвҖҷs Fight to Break the Cycle
summary: AshleeвҖҷs story is one of unimaginable loss, resilience, and transformation. Growing up as the daughter of parents who struggled with addiction, Ashlee faced emotional trauma, instability, and deep grief from an early age. After losing both her mother and father, she continued to navigate life while raising her children, working as a social service worker, and supporting other women through trauma, domestic violence, and motherhood. In this episode of For the Hayters, Ashlee opens up about her childhood, the grief of losing her parents, the strength it took to finish school while heartbroken, and the work she does today to help others break free from the cycles of addiction and pain. This conversation is a powerful reminder that healing is possible, even when the wounds run deep. title: Episode 134: A WarriorвҖҷs Journey: Councillor HilariaвҖҷs Fight for Life, Hope, and Advocacy
podcast_name: Diary of a Kidney Warrior Podcast (Kidney Health Podcast)
summary: In this deeply moving episode of The Diary of a Kidney Warrior Podcast, Dee sits down with the inspirational Councillor Hilaria as she shares her extraordinary journey through chronic kidney disease, multiple organ failures, and ultimately, a life-saving transplant. Her story is one of unimaginable adversityвҖ”from being diagnosed during pregnancy to surviving septicaemia, cardiac arrest, and a coma. Yet, through it all, Councillor Hilaria never gave up. В  She candidly recounts her years on both peritoneal and haemodialysis, the emotional toll of waiting a decade for a transplant, and the strength she drew from her loving husband, children and dedicated healthcare team. When the call finally came, it wasnвҖҷt just a kidneyвҖ”it was a second chance at life. Councillor Hilaria reflects on the profound gratitude she feels for her donorвҖҷs family and how this experience reignited her faith and purpose. В  Now an elected official and powerful advocate for kidney health and organ donation, Councillor HilariaвҖҷs story is a beacon of resilience, transformation, and service. Whether youвҖҷre a kidney warrior, caregiver, or ally, this episode offers hope, strength, and a reminder that even in our darkest moments, there is light ahead. title: Damon Hill: Carrying His Father's Legacy Through F1вҖҷs Most Ruthless Era (E352)
summary: Damon Hill is a former Formula 1 World Champion and one of the most thoughtful voices in motorsport. The son of legendary racer Graham Hill, Damon carved out his own legacy in a sport shaped by speed, risk, and legacy. In this episode, he opens up with a rare mix of honesty and introspection, sharing stories that go far beyond the racetrack. The conversation dives into DamonвҖҷs memories of Ayrton Senna, not just as a rival, but as a human being. He reflects on SennaвҖҷs deep sense of duty to Brazil, his kindness off the track, and the emotional shockwave that followed his tragic accident. ItвҖҷs a raw, moving look at what it meant to lose someone who carried so much weight for an entire nation. Damon also gets personal about the influence of his father, Graham Hill. He talks about growing up in the shadow of greatness, navigating grief, and eventually finding his own path in the same high-stakes world.В  This episode offers a heartfelt exploration of the human side of racing, with Damon sharing honest lessons on family, legacy, and overcoming adversity that will resonate with fans and newcomers alike. title: The Use of Psilocybin for End of Life Care and Why Meditation and Breathwork Work to Manage Stress with Psychologist Shanae O'Leary
summary: TodayвҖҷs episode is one that I have wanted to create since January, when mum was told there was no more treatment and my family and I had to dive deep into death. I know no one likes to talk about it, but I actually want to change the narrative and become more open about this topic. Grieving is the most common human experience, everyone will have to endure it at some point so understanding end of life care a little better has empowered me and really helped with the healing journey.В  On the podcast I have Shanae OвҖҷLeary, a registered psychologist who has a strong passion in palliative care treatments. Shanae has done a lot of work on trauma and is currently working with a team of health professional and palliative care specialists to make psilocybin assisted therapy accessible to people given a terminal diagnosis who are experiencing existential distress towards the end of their life.В  More information about End Wise below: https://www.endwise.au/

title: How to Thrive Through Menopause: My Journey & Essential Health Tips
podcast_name: All About You With Dr Shauna Watts
summary: In this eye-opening episode, Dr. Shawna opens up about her unique menopause journey after undergoing surgical menopause during a cesarean section. She candidly discusses the dramatic hormonal shifts she experienced and how they impacted her mental clarity, mood, and overall health. Drawing from both her personal story and medical expertise, Dr. Shawna breaks down the wide range of menopause and perimenopause symptoms вҖ” from brain fog and anxiety to weight gain, sleep challenges, and changes in libido.What to ExpectвҖў Understanding surgical menopause and hormone changesвҖў The reality of brain fog and cognitive symptoms in menopauseвҖў How HRT can help and when to consider itвҖў Practical tips for improving sleep hygieneвҖў Managing menopause symptoms naturally and proactivelyвҖў The emotional and physical challenges women face during perimenopause and beyondWhether youвҖҷre newly navigating perimenopause or deep into menopause, this conversation offers valuable insights, practical advice on hormone replacement therapy (HRT), and lifestyle strategies to help you reclaim your energy, confidence, and well-being.This episode is brought to you by RUKSAK - helping you turn every walk into a workout with weighted vests, backpacks, and accessories. title: Episode 272 Karen Coleman -Ostrov Five years living an Intermittent Fasting Lifestyle and Never Looking back.
summary: Karen is 65 years old and has tried justabout every diet you can think of вҖ“ The Scarsdale Diet, Atkins and keto diets, Weight Watchers, Noom, the Cabbage Soup, Healthy Inspirations, grapefruit diet, Whole30, Slim-Fast, South Beach, Green Smoothie Diet, Nutrisystem, hCG вҖ“ if youвҖҷve heard of it, Karen probably tried it!В В   Some of these diets brought Karen temporarysuccess, and others caused her to gain weight or slow her metabolism.В  None of them were sustainable, and the weightalways crept back. One day in the summer of 2020, Karen was perusing Facebook and saw a photo of her sister-in-law, Dedra Campbell.В  Karen thought Dedra had posted a вҖңThrowback ThursdayвҖқ photo, as her sister-in-law looked young, slim, and glowing.В  Karen was shocked to learn the photo was recent, so she immediately called her sister-in-law to find out what she did to lose weight and seemingly age backwards.В  Dedra told her the secret was IntermittentFasting brought up negative thoughts and feelings in Karen.В  In KarenвҖҷs mind, fasting = deprivation and hunger.В  However, with her sister-in-lawвҖҷs encouragement, she listened to the basics of the program and bought the book she recommended, вҖңFast.Feast.Repeat.вҖқ by Gin Stephens.В  Karen read the book and said she would try it for one day to see how it went.В  On August 13, 2020, Karen did a clean 18/6 fast, found it to be effortless, and the rest, as they say, is history.В   Karen was invited to GraemeвҖҷs group, The Fasting Highway, by Dedra after another IF guru closed her Facebook group and moved to another platform.В  Karen had been fasting for not quite a year when she joined The Fasting Highway.В  Karen had only lost 10 pounds by this time, but was encouraged and inspired by Graeme, his book, and fellow group members to stay the course and trust the process. Karen realised that her weight loss journey was her own, and she could not compare herself to others. Karen is 5вҖҷ5вҖқ and her highest weight ever was 208 pounds.В  She could not bend over to tie her shoes at that weight and was always out of breath.В  She also suffered from sleep apnea.В  KarenвҖҷs IF starting weight was 175.4 pounds, and she set her goal weight at 160 pounds (because she didnвҖҷt think that at herage, being post-menopausal, having T2D, and being insulin resistant, she could get below that weight).В  However,on July 3, 2023, Karen weighed 149 pounds, a weight she hadnвҖҷt seen in almost 20 years.В  Best thing вҖ“ there wasno struggle to get there!В  Yes, it did take almost 3 years to lose the weight, but the point is вҖ“ she lost it withintermittent fasting when nothing else worked!В  ItвҖҷs now been almost 5 years since Karen started intermittent fasting, and her current weight is 142 pounds.В  She sometimes gets as low as 138 pounds, bouncing between that number and 142 pounds.В  Karen went from a size 12 (US) to wearing sizes 4 and 6.В  Intermittent fasting gave Karen theconfidence that she would never have to lose weight again вҖ“ and for the first time in years, she doesnвҖҷt have 5 different clothing sizes in her closet.В  She donated all her вҖңbigвҖқ clothes to charity, knowing sheвҖҷd never need them again.В   Another personal win is that Karen decidedto embrace her greys and, as a result, cut all the hair colour out.В  She would never have had theconfidence to do this at her higher weights! To join the Patreon Community.  Please go to www.patreon.com/thefastinghighway or visit the website www.thefastinghighway.com for more information. title: Episode 214: What can we do to prevent bowel cancer in young people?
summary: Can Gut Health in Childhood Help Prevent Bowel Cancer Later in Life? This week on the podcast, weвҖҷre talking about something that might feel a little confronting but also incredibly empowering: how our kidsвҖҷ health today could shape their future health tomorrow. Bowel cancer rates are on the rise in young people, and new research is pointing to a surprising contributor вҖ” one weвҖҷve been talking about for a long time here at Natural Super Kids: gut health.В In this episode, we will break down what this means for parents without fear, but with practical action in mind. HereвҖҷs what we cover: What the latest research says about childhood gut bacteria and bowel cancer risk.How early-life factors like C-section births, antibiotics, and diet can impact long-term gut health.Subtle signs that your childвҖҷs gut may be out of balance вҖ” from tummy troubles to mood changes.Easy, gut-supportive changes you can start making right now (even if life feels busy).рҹҺ§ Tune in to learn how small steps like adding variety to meals or spending more time outside can make a big difference to your childвҖҷs health, both now and in the years to come. title: Natural vs Prescription: Which Appetite Strategy For Weight Loss Really Works?
summary: Show Notes: Episode Summary: In this episode, Rick dives deep into one of the most talked-about trends in weight loss today: GLP-1вҖ“based appetite control.  With prescription options grabbing headlines and natural alternatives gaining ground, how do you decide whatвҖҷs right for you? YouвҖҷll learn what GLP-1 actually does, why it's so effective at curbing cravings, and how both the clinical and natural routes tap the same hunger hormoneвҖ”just in very different ways.  Whether youвҖҷre looking for rapid results or sustainable change, this episode gives you the tools to choose your path with confidence. Key Takeaways: GLP-1 is a gut hormone that tells your brain youвҖҷre full. Both prescription meds and natural foods can raise it.Prescription GLP-1 injections work fastвҖ”but come with high costs and common side effects. Nausea, muscle loss, and rebound appetite are real risks.Natural GLP-1 support through fiber, fermented foods, and plant compounds offers a gentler approach. ItвҖҷs slower but more sustainable, with bonus benefits like gut health and muscle preservation.The best path depends on your budget, timeline, and tolerance for trade-offs. One isnвҖҷt betterвҖ”itвҖҷs about alignment with your lifestyle.The Natural GLP-1 Stack offers a real-world, food-first strategyвҖ”including psyllium, kimchi, berberine, resistant starch, and strength training for lasting appetite control. Pick your pathвҖ”then commit to it for six consistent weeks.В  If youвҖҷre going the natural route, start with one habit from the GLP-1 stack today.

title: More Than Footy: Paddy McCartin on Owning Type 1 and Its Biggest Life Lessons
summary: When Paddy McCartin was diagnosed with type 1 diabetes at just eight years old, his world shiftedвҖ”but his passion for footy never wavered. Despite ongoing battles with diabetes and a career-ending series of concussions, Paddy carved out a remarkable AFL journey that took him from Geelong to the Sydney Swans and back into the hearts of the diabetes community. In this episode, Paddy opens up about the highs and lows of elite sport with type 1, how technology transformed his management, and the power of letting people in. From the locker rooms to life after retirement, this is Paddy like youвҖҷve never heard him beforeвҖ”raw, reflective, and real. HereвҖҷs what weвҖҷre covering: -The early signs of type 1 and how an 8-year-old learned to self-manage with stubborn independence -Why finger pricks were PaddyвҖҷs most hated partвҖ”and how he eventually overcame that barrier -The вҖңcarrotвҖқ that got him back on a pump and chasing his AFL dream -The real reason he kept his diabetes quiet in footy circlesвҖ”and the moment he finally opened up -How everything changed with a single speech to the Swans playing group -Game day routines, insulin strategies and the tech hacks that helped him thrive on the field -The bittersweet reality of early retirementвҖ”and how he found purpose in giving back -What heвҖҷd say to his diabetes if it were a person (spoiler: itвҖҷs not all bad) вҖҰ and a whole lot more Chapters: вҸі [00:00] вҖңI Just DidnвҖҷt Want to Play FootyвҖқ: The Diagnosis That Changed Everything вҸі [01:59] Meet Paddy McCartin: Footy Star, Type 1 Role Model, Reluctant Retiree вҸі [07:10] First Symptoms, First Needles, and an 8-Year-OldвҖҷs Determination вҸі [13:05] Rebel with a Cause: Fighting Against Diabetes Burnout вҸі [19:10] Back on the Pump: The Deal Paddy Made to Chase the AFL Dream вҸі [25:22] Hidden Fears and Public Perception: Why Paddy Stayed Quiet вҸі [28:07] The Swans, the Speech, and the Shift in Identity вҸі [34:16] From Draft Night to Diabetes Tech: How It All Played Out вҸі [36:13] Game Day Strategy: Insulin, Oats, Temp Targets and Gels вҸі [45:11] Playing with Tech: Pumps on the Sideline, Physios with Phones вҸі [50:11] Post-Match Highs, Hypos and Recovery Routines вҸі [55:21] Sydney Swans and a Second Chance: The Comeback Chapter вҸі [58:30] Forced Retirement, Lasting Gratitude, and a Whole Lot of Perspective вҸі [01:04:41] Pilates, Weights and Wiser Habits: How Paddy Moves Today вҸі [01:10:14] Diet, Fasting and Feeling Good in 2025 вҸі [01:16:08] The Magic Wand Question: Would He Cure His Diabetes? вҸі [01:20:10] The One Lesson HeвҖҷd Pass on to Every Young Type 1 Hope you enjoy! title: Floored! Episode 76 with Dr Sara Chan Reardon
summary: Hello & Welcome to today's episode where I had the great pleasure of chatting with my friend & colleague, Dr Sara Chan Reardon, about her new book, Floored! (out June 10th) рҹҘірҹҘірҹҘі Sara has built not only a successful clinical career but also a huge social media presence delivering pelvic health education, and now sheвҖҷs in her writer era - her new book Floored! is now available for pre-order (with some extra goodies if you sign up via thevagwhisperer.com In our conversation рҹ“қWe discussed the challenges of transitioning from a clinical background to writing and the importance of educating women about all aspects of their pelvic health at every life stage, from menstrual health through perinatal to menopause and beyond, including bladder, bowel and pain issues рҹ“қSara also emphasised the need for a comprehensive approach that includes both in-person therapy and online resources. She shared her experiences as a pelvic health pysiotherapist, highlighting the challenges of reaching patients and the importance of demystifying pelvic health issues.  рҹ“қShe also discussed the challenges of writing a book on the topic, including balancing technical information with accessibility for a wider audience & the conversation also touched on the importance of empowering women to advocate for themselves in medical settings and the need for a more preventative approach to pelvic health. рҹ“қIвҖҷve had the pleasure reading an advance copy of SaraвҖҷs book вҖҳFlooredвҖҷ and I think itвҖҷs going to be so valuable to both women who are interested in learning more about pelvic health at every age & stage but also to clinicians who are looking for accessible language and strategies to educate and empower women  Pre-order your copy today at your local bookshop! title: Sober Shift: How One Question Changed Everything
summary: Send us a text In this episode, I chat with sober coach and father, Luke Jones, to unpack what it really takes to ditch the bottle and build a life you donвҖҷt want to escape from. Luke opens up about his own journeyвҖ”from regular heavy drinking shaped by British pub culture, to the quiet but powerful wake-up call that came from his young sonвҖҷs simple question. What followed was a deep, honest transformationвҖ”one that led Luke to sobriety, coaching, and helping other dads rewrite their stories. We explore the emotional and physical clarity that comes with quitting alcohol, the difference between being an alcoholic and simply having a problem with drinking, and why waiting for rock bottom isnвҖҷt necessaryвҖ”or wise. This is a conversation about mindset, fatherhood, environment, and building non-negotiable habits that support real change. From gratitude journaling to manifestation, Luke shares practical tools that helped himвҖ”and now help othersвҖ”reclaim their time, their energy, and their role as a positive influence for the next generation. title: A Lighter Body, A Louder Voice: What We Gained by Losing almost 200lbs [S2E8]
podcast_name: The Dose with Paddy and Belinda
summary: We lost almost 100 pounds eachвҖ”but spoiler alert: we didnвҖҷt magically become perfect humans. In this laugh-through-the-tears episode, Paddy and Belinda unpack the wild, wonderful, and downright weird things that happen after weight loss. From squeezing into Paul Mescal-style OвҖҷNeills shorts to Belinda strutting around in mustard mini skirts (with a zip, obviously), we share whatвҖҷs changedвҖ”from food guilt to fashion fitsвҖ”and what still hits us hard (hello, body image baggage and funeral rosary anxiety). Paddy revisits a letter he once read on The Biggest Loser and reflects on the pressure to be вҖңfixed.вҖқ Belinda speaks about showing up for herself and finally not caring if she ever steps on a scale again. We talk about protecting our peace, ditching cold shoulder tops, and finding joy in a full-fat ice cream with syrup and a flakeвҖ”without spiralling into self-hate. This episode is full of big laughs, deep sighs, awkward honesty, and hard-won freedom. If youвҖҷve ever felt less-than, tried to shrink yourself, or wondered what it might feel like to finally take up spaceвҖ”this oneвҖҷs for you. P.S. WeвҖҷre not fixed. But weвҖҷre f***ing free. ======= If you enjoy this podcast please follow / subscribe, download, and leave us a review.В  If you don't enjoy this podcast............ well this is awks.В  Combined Paddy & Belindas weight loss totals over 190lbs supported by GLP1 Medications Ozempic & Mounjaro along with a focus on nutrition, movement and associated lifestyle changes. None of the content in this episode is to be treated as medical advice or promotion towards any specific treatment or medication - this is a sharing of a personal journey. title: Imagine a public health system built on mutual aid, participation, and dignity with Eric Reinhart
summary: CareQuest Institute for Oral Health is a national nonprofit dedicated to creating an oral health care system that is accessible, equitable, and integrated. Learn more about how their advocacy, philanthropy, research, and education are creating a better oral health system at carequest.org/turnonthelights What if the path to better health and stronger communities doesnвҖҷt start in hospitals, but in our relationships with each other? In this episode, Dr. Eric Reinhart, a political anthropologist, social psychiatrist, and psychoanalytic clinician based in Chicago, reflects on how his experiences in medicine, anthropology, and advocacy have shaped his urgent focus on public health reform, especially around mass incarceration and its devastating effects on communities. He argues that incarceration is not only a symptom but also a cause of widespread mental illness and social breakdown, emphasizing the need to democratize caregiving and public health beyond bureaucratic systems. Reinhart critiques the U.S. health care model for being overly medicalized and profit-driven, calling for a bottom-up approach that empowers communities to care for one another. He warns against relying on political self-correction, stressing that meaningful change requires validating public anger and offering materially supported, ethical alternatives rooted in relational care. Drawing from models like Paul FarmerвҖҷs work and his own experiences in underserved communities, Reinhart envisions a public health system built on mutual aid, participation, and dignity. Tune in as Dr. Eric Reinhart challenges us to rethink public health by examining how systems of incarceration and inequality undermine care and what we can do to build something better! title: The Exact Steps to Lose the First 30lbs (I Would Do These if I had to Start Over)
summary: I lost 100 pounds 16 years ago, but what if I had to start all over?  Today, I'm giving you "The Exact Steps To Lose the First 30 Pounds (I would do these if I had to start over)".  And I think you'll be surprised how much of it has NOTHING to do with food.  Inside the episode, I break it down into 3 phases, and each one might surprise you:   First 10 pounds: I wouldn't change anything about what I was eating. I'd focus on 4 simple things instead Second 10 pounds: I'd track a few hidden eating patterns (once you see them, you'll never look at food the same way) Final 10 pounds: Only NOW would I pay attention to food. I wouldn't cut anything out, but maybe make some simple swaps.  Want to know exactly what I'd do to lose 30 pounds? Listen now.  Most of us fail diets because we try to make a million changes on day one. I did that for YEARS.  What finally worked was taking baby steps that felt doable - even when my brain screamed, "that's not enough!"  The scale can go down while your quality of life goes up. It's not just possible - it's the only way to lose weight and keep it off.  Get the Free Course here: http://NoBSFreeCourse.com

title: Unlocking habit change. The neuroscience behind habits with Jess Wall from Chimp Management
summary: In this conversation, host Lynette interviews Jess Wall, a psychological skills mentor from CHIMP Management, discussing the CHIMP model developed by Professor Steve Peters.  Jess introduces herself and her role, elaborating on how the CHIMP model helps understand human behavior by exploring the neuroscience behind it. She shares her personal journey to joining CHIMP Management and explains various aspects of the model, including the interaction between the CHIMP and human parts of the brain, and how they impact habits and behaviors.  The discussion covers practical applications, such as behaviour change, procrastination, and habit formation, emphasising the importance of self-compassion, planning, and understanding one's chimp to achieve lasting change.  The episode concludes with Jess providing resources and recommendations for utilising CHIMP Management's tools and courses to improve mental well-being.

title: Lose weight without dieting, with Emma Bardwell
summary: Fed up with crash diets and fad weight loss advice? Registered nutritionist Emma Bardwell joins Liz to reveal how we can better manage our weight in midlife - without dieting or restriction. Liz and Emma discuss dealing with body changes in menopause, strategies for bloating, and whether we can really treat menopause symptoms through diet alone. The episode also takes a deep dive into the calorie counting debate, how we can hit our fibre, protein and plant points for fat loss, plus Emma shares her recipes to make hitting these goals easier. title: Want to Export Your Business?
summary: This episode of High Performance is in partnership with The Department for Business and Trade (DBT) and The UK Export Academy. In the special episode, Jake Humphrey leads a powerful roundtable conversation exploring the real challenges and rewards of expanding internationally.В  Featuring voices from the UK Export Academy and a range of inspiring UK entrepreneurs, the episode dives into practical advice, cultural insights, and personal stories from those who've successfully taken their businesses abroad. The Department for Business and Trade (DBT) is the UK governmentвҖҷs economic growth department, supporting businesses to invest, export and grow, creating jobs and opportunities across the country. The UK Export Academy is a free training program designed to help UK businesses expand into international markets, increasing their reach and profitability by tapping into new international customers. If you would like to know anything else about taking your business global, or to sign up to the free UK Export Academy, click the link here: great.gov.uk/campaign-site/global This episode features Mark Burkett, Trade Advisor at the UK Export Academy, Raj Theper, Adviser at the UK Export Academy, Laura Ward, founder of EXEAT, Lyndsay Mitcheson, founder of Neo Walk, Saul Parker, co-founder and CEO of The Good Side, and Elizabeth Lamb, Managing Director of The Data Escape Room.

title: 121: Breakups in Midlife: When Menopause Reshapes Your Relationship
summary: What happens to marriages and long-term relationships during menopause?В  Clinical psychologist Kirstin Bouse returns to the podcast to tackle this powerful question, revealing why 70% of midlife breakups are initiated by women. The menopause transition triggers not just hormonal changes but profound psychological shifts. Neuroplasticity during this period reshapes women's brains, often leading to significant evolutions in identity, values, and what they're willing to tolerate. As Kirstin explains, "I hear from a lot of women: I just want to live from me, not from my role as mum or wife. I want to experience joy that's just about me." We explore how these changes impact relationships from multiple angles. For partnerships with potential, we discuss how honest conversations can determine whether couples can evolve together or if paths must diverge.В  Importantly we discuss that when a woman is living in an unhealthy or controlling relationship, perimenopause may increase vulnerability making it even more important that her safety and wellbeing are the top priority. The conversation doesn't shy away from the challenges of rebuilding life as a single person in midlife, with Kirstin offering practical guidance on finding joy, creating a new identity, and building resilience. Throughout it all, one theme remains constant: the importance of support during this transformative period. Whether you're questioning your own relationship, supporting a friend through changes, or simply wanting to understand this common midlife phenomenon, this episode offers insight, compassion, and a roadmap for navigating relationships through the turbulence of menopause. Subscribe to Dear Menopause for more expert conversations that help you navigate this significant life transition with knowledge and confidence. If you or someone you know is experiencing an unsafe or abusive relationship, please seek support - help is available, and you are not alone. Support services within Australia are listed below. Links Kirstin's website All About Her Centre Relationships Australia Dept of Social Services Mission Australia 1800RESPECT Thank you for listening to my show!  Join the conversation on Instagram

title: From Conflict to Connection: Transforming Relationships Through the Enneagram with Christa Hardin, MA
summary: In this episode of Typology, we take a look at the fascinating intersection of the Enneagram and relationships with our special guest, Crista Hardin, a relationship expert and author of "The Enneagram in Marriage." Christa, an Enneagram 7, brings her vibrant energy and insights to the conversation, discussing how her type influences her marriage and her work with couples.  We explore the complexities of anxiety in Sevens, the importance of understanding core fears in relationships, and how different Enneagram types interact with one another. Christa shares her personal experiences, including her journey through marriage and the challenges she and her husband have faced over nearly 30 years together.  Throughout the episode, we touch on the dynamics of various type pairings, the challenges and transformations that can arise in relationships, and the significance of emotional regulation and understanding in conflict resolution. Christa emphasizes the importance of recognizing our own flaws and the value of shared meaning in relationships.  Join us for this enlightening discussion that not only sheds light on the Enneagram but also offers practical insights for navigating the complexities of love and partnership. Don't forget to check out Christa's podcast, "Enneagram and Marriage," and her website for more resources! title: #38 Re-thinking Exercise:  New Evidence, Smarter Moves
podcast_name: Live Long and Well with Dr. Bobby
summary: Send us a text Take the Health Type Quiz or Join the Newsletter here Please give me podcast feedback with a few questions here In this episode, I revisit the most powerful tool for longevityвҖ”exerciseвҖ”and unpack fresh research that changes how we should think about its timing, type, and impact. We begin with a quick recap of the foundational elements covered in Episode 2, emphasizing that aerobic activity can lower the risk of mortality, heart attack, stroke, cancer, and dementia by 20вҖ“30%, even when started later in life.В  Then we explore compelling new studies that show how exercise affects everything from blood pressure to brain volume. A meta-analysis of 14,000 participants shows even 15вҖ“20 minutes of moderate exercise daily lowers blood pressure. A review of 19 trials links physical activity to better sleep efficiency and reduced wake time. In terms of weight management, a meta-analysis of 116 randomized trials finds that even 30 minutes of exercise weekly leads to measurable weight loss, with a dose-response up to 300 minutes per week. Exercise also appears to influence mood. A review of 33 observational studies involving nearly 100,000 adults suggests that 5,000вҖ“7,000 steps daily correlate with reduced depressive symptoms. While causality remains uncertain, the association is intriguing. Beyond mental health, strength training may reduce inflammation: 19 trials show CRP levels dropped inВ  adults who engaged in resistance training. Perhaps most excitingly, a smaller study found higher cardiorespiratory fitness linked to greater hippocampal volume and better memory in older adults. To optimize these benefits, we explore emerging science around exercise timing and routine structure. A large study of 14,000 users wearing devices found that intense workouts within two hours of bedtime delayed sleep onset by 36 minutes. Perhaps finish workouts at least four hours before sleep for better rest. Interestingly, data from over 400,000 adults reveals that women gain more longevity benefit from exercise than menвҖ”and with less time invested. We also cover weekend warriors. A UK Biobank study showed no difference in health outcomes based on when exercise occurred, debunking the belief that daily consistency is essentialвҖ”what matters is that you do it. If you're sleep-deprived, caffeine might restore performance, as shown in a 10K time trial study. Lastly, a novel study using post-workout cold exposure showed that cold immersion reduced blood flow and amino acid uptake for up to 3 hours.

title: Episode 50 - How social pressure can affect your health choices and what to do about it.
summary: One of things many of my accountability clients struggle with is the social pressure from others to step out of their health commitments.В  В This often looks like consuming food or alcohol that is not aligned with their health goals.В  В I have experienced this myself and know how tough it can be.В В  В  This podcast addresses the 'good' and 'bad' social pressures and then gives some strategies to help with sticking to your goals when others try and derail you...В  How to respond and stand up for what you believe in: Find like-minded people Explain with confidence and be assertive - no thanks and not for me Think of an excuse Suggest something else to do Avoid certain situations Stand up for others Ask questions of the other person вҖ“ deflect Be repetitive Consider the results of giving in Seek support вҖ“ therapy В  Enjoy and thank you for listening..

title: Rock Bottom to Redemption with Niall Harbison
summary: Send us a text "What if your darkest moment became the catalyst for your greatest transformation?" This question lies at the heart of my profound conversation with Niall Harbison, whose remarkable journey from corporate success to rock-bottom alcoholism to purposeful revival might just change your perspective on what truly matters in life. Niall's story begins as a private chef for billionaires aboard luxury yachts, where million-dollar fuel bills and private jets delivering Italian cheese were commonplace. He witnessed firsthand the hollow nature of extreme wealth before returning to Dublin to build his own successful businesses. Yet beneath this veneer of achievement lurked depression, anxiety, and a growing dependence on alcohol. When COVID struck, Niall's life spiraled into a six-week bender in Thailand that nearly killed him. Lying in a hospital bed, certain he was dying, he experienced a revelation that would transform everything: the corporate success, social media followers, and material possessions he'd chased meant absolutely nothing. What flashed through his mind instead were simple moments of childhood joy and connection. That rock-bottom moment became what Niall now calls "the best thing in my life." Upon recovery, he discovered purpose in rescuing Thailand's street dogs. What began as feeding a few hungry canines has expanded into a charity that feeds 1,200 dogs daily, performs thousands of sterilizations monthly, and has built a hospital named after Tina, a special rescue who lived just six months in freedom after a lifetime of abuse. Niall's transformation at age 41 stands as powerful testament that it's never too late to reinvent yourself. His advice resonates with brutal honesty: "Follow your own path. Take risks. You don't have to do the life that's mapped out for you." Whether you're questioning your purpose or just need a reminder of what truly matters, this conversation might be exactly what you need to hear. Subscribe now and join the thousands finding inspiration in these authentic human stories of transformation. Thanks for listening! You can watch the full episode on YouTube here. title: The Marathon Mindset вҖ“ Raising Children with T1D for the Long Run
summary: In this motivating and down-to-earth episode, Catherine explores what it means to parent a child with type 1 diabetes (T1D) with a marathon mindset rather than a sprint mentality. She challenges the pressures of achieving perfect blood sugar numbers and warns against the unsustainable, all-or-nothing approaches often fuelled by social media and fear. Instead, she offers a practical, compassionate framework using SMART goalsвҖ”Specific, Measurable, Achievable, Realistic, and TimelyвҖ”to encourage gradual, joyful, and sustainable diabetes management. Listeners are encouraged to shift the focus from perfection to progress, helping children learn patience, resilience, and flexibility. Key messages include: Diabetes is a lifelong journey, not a race to perfect numbers.All-or-nothing thinking can lead to guilt and burnout.SMART goals can support steady improvement without overwhelming children.Joy and balance matter вҖ” food and life should be celebrated, not restricted.Self-compassion and role-modelling are essential to raising empowered, healthy kids with T1D.CatherineвҖҷs closing message reminds parents that imperfect days are normalвҖ”and that raising a child with diabetes is not about getting everything right, but about staying in it for the long run with love, grace, and determination. The Diabetic Mammy is the heartfelt, no-nonsense podcast where type 1 diabetes meets motherhood, community, and a whole lot of real talk. Hosted by Catherine, a mammy navigating the highs and lows of type 1 diabetes, each episode blends personal insight with practical wisdom, offering support for parents, carers, and anyone living with or loving someone with diabetes. From tech transitions and hospital diagnoses to school lunches and stolen sleep, this podcast embraces the messy, meaningful moments that make up life with chronic illness. Whether youвҖҷre newly diagnosed or a seasoned warrior, The Diabetic Mammy is your space to feel seen, supported, and never alone. Connect with Catherine by email on catherine@abundantlydiabetes.com or reach her on Instagram: abundantlydiabetes  Hosted on Acast. See acast.com/privacy for more information.

title: The Consequences of Blast Wave Action on Young People
summary: In this episode of the Making Vestibular Visible podcast we talk about blast wave injuries, and the vestibular injury that results from near- or far- exposure to explosives. Whether due to an isolated act of terrorism or an ongoing regional conflict, the impact of explosives is severe. It's estimated that 1 in 8 people globally have been exposed to conflict in 2024. This includes those living within 5 kilometers (3 miles) of violent events, such as bombings and explosions. The ongoing conflict in Ukraine is one of the places where soldiers and civilians experience war-related injuries.В  Speaker: вҖӢDr. Konstantin Trinus is a distinguished medical professional from Kyiv, Ukraine specializing in neuro-otology. He graduated with honors from Kyiv Medical Institute and earned his PhD focusing on human and animal physiology. He has a Doctor of Medicine degree in neurology, with a dissertation on vestibular disturbances as early indicators of neurological deficits in individuals exposed to ionizing radiation from the Chornobyl catastrophe. Dr. Trinus has held various research and academic positions and has lectured internationally, authored numerous scientific publications, and received several awards, such as the Tsiolkovsky Medal and the Claussen-Tato Prize in 2018 for his significant contributions to neuro-otology. вҖӢВ  Dr. Trinus has conducted extensive research into the vestibular consequences of blast wave injuries, particularly among participants of the Anti-Terrorist Operation (ATO) in Ukraine. His studies have identified a significant prevalence of vestibular disturbances in individuals exposed to blast trauma, including symptoms such as dizziness, vertigo, kinetosis, and orthostatic discomfort. For instance, his research revealed that 70.77% of ATO participants reported dizziness, and 26.15% experienced objective vertigo, indicating a strong correlation between blast exposure and vestibular dysfunction. His work emphasizes the need for comprehensive vestibular assessment and targeted rehabilitation strategies for individuals affected by blast-induced mild traumatic brain injuries. вҖӢ

title: The Whole Truth About Supplements: Why Wholefoods Work When Synthetics DonвҖҷt вҖ“ with Sheridan Joy Austin
podcast_name: The Wellness Collective with Nat Kringoudis
summary: LetвҖҷs talk about the supplement industryвҖ”and why so much of it is failing women. In this raw, uplifting and incredibly educational conversation, IвҖҷm joined by Sheridan Joy Austin, the beautiful mind and founder behind Foraged For You, to dive into what it really takes to nourish our bodiesвҖ”especially in pregnancy, postpartum, and perimenopause. Sheridan opens up about how her own unexpected pregnancy revealed the gaps in prenatal nutrition and how that led to the creation of her now cult-favourite wholefood blend, The MotherвҖҷs Blend. We talk liver, folate, iron, vitamin A, and why isolating nutrients in synthetic supplements often creates more harm than good. Sheridan shares her journey of bringing a product to life with true integrity, the resistance sheвҖҷs faced, and why she refuses to cut cornersвҖ”even when the wellness industry tells her to. This episode is for any woman whoвҖҷs ever questioned her supplements, felt overwhelmed by mixed health advice, or just wants to understand how to truly fuel her body with the wisdom of nature. Key Takeaways Why real food always winsвҖ”how nutrient-dense ingredients like liver and kelp naturally support pregnancy, postpartum, and hormone recovery better than synthetics. What your supplements arenвҖҷt telling youвҖ”why isolating nutrients like folic acid or iron can backfire without their critical co-factors like vitamin A, copper, and magnesium. How to read the fine printвҖ”what hidden fillers like maltodextrin and silicon dioxide are doing to your gut, and how to choose products with real integrity. Why iron deficiency might not be about ironвҖ”how symptoms like fatigue and poor immunity may be rooted in deeper imbalances most blood tests donвҖҷt show. The truth about vitamin A in pregnancyвҖ”why liver-based vitamin A isnвҖҷt the enemy, and what the research really says about birth defects and deficiency risks. title: STOP Ignoring Premature Ovarian Insufficiency Symptoms!

summary: In this insightful episode, we explore Premature Ovarian Insufficiency (POI), a condition affecting women under the age of 40 that leads to a decline in ovarian function and fertility. title: Myofascial Magic + The Bridge of Metaphor with Joanne Avison
summary: In this episode, we explore the art of living, moving, sensing, and becoming with todayвҖҷs guest, the brilliant and deeply insightful Dr. Joanne Avison, author of Myofascial Magic in Action.  In this soul-stirring conversation, Joanne takes us on a journey through the living terrain of the body, inviting us to rediscover ourselvesвҖ”not as machines, but as unified beings of body, mind, emotions, and spirit.  Joanne calls metaphor a bridgeвҖ”a sacred structure that carries us from the realm of abstract thought into the embodied wisdom of lived experience. Through this bridge, we navigate what I call Yin-sight: the subtle feminine way of seeing and sensing, rooted in fluidity, sensation, and stillness.  Together, we reflect on:    What it means to live in a world that still defines the body through the mechanistic metaphors of the past.    The archetypal story of Pinocchio, and how Myofascial Magic in Action offers us all the wish to finally be whole.    How metaphor reveals the magic within the mundane, the sacred in the simple, and the healing hidden in the body's knowing.    Joanne shares her collaborations with leading thinkers and somatic pioneers and challenges us to reconsider the very paradigms that have shaped anatomy and movement education for over 400 years.  This episode is a call to awakenвҖ”a call to feel more, to see differently, and to remember that the body is not a thing, but a living story. Enter the realm where science meets soul, where form meets function, and where the mundane becomes magical. title: Midlife Fueling 101: Essential Sports Nutrition Advice for Peri & Postmenopausal Women with Kristen Arnold, MS, RDN, CSSD (Episode 226)
summary: Muscle and bone loss. Inflammation. Bloating, diarrhea, and other GI issues. Peri and postmenopausal active women can face myriad training and performance challenges. But the good news is that a lot of them can be helped with proper nutrition practices. This week we dive into the basics on how to fuel your active midlife body, including what to know about carbohydrates, ultraprocessed foods, energy availability, protein, anti-inflammatory foods, supplements, and more with sports dietitian Kristen Arnold.В  Kristen Arnold MS, RDN, CSSD is a board-certified sports dietitian, cycling coach, retired professional athlete, and team sport director for USA Cycling and professional cycling teams. Her private practice is centered around empowering women through evidence-based sports nutrition coaching and information, and she has done research on improving postmenopausal women's health through nutrition strategies and counseling. title: Episode 45: Ivy League scientist Dr. Lisa Miller on consciousness, the brain & the nature of reality
summary: Dr. Lisa Miller is a psychologist and scientist from Columbia University, widely regarded as the leading authority on the science of spirituality. While science and spirituality are often seen as opposites, Dr. MillerвҖҷs groundbreaking research reveals that the two are deeply interconnected.  Her work has shown that we are actually biologically hardwired for spirituality. So itвҖҷs no surprise her contributions have earned critical acclaim amongst fellow scientists and the media. In 2014, Dr. MillerвҖҷs TED Talk went viral, racking up 2.6 million views, where she demonstrated how spirituality can act as a protective shield against depression. Since then, her work has been featured by Big Think, The New York Times, The Wall Street Journal, TIME, and NBC.  SheвҖҷs also authored two bestselling books вҖ“ The Spiritual Child and The Awakened Brain.  In this episode, we dive deep into the research, exploring how science has proven our spiritual nature. Dr. Miller unpacks some really fascinating findings, including the role of synchronicity, the connection between spirituality and a sense of purpose, and why having something greater than yourself in life is essential.  We also discuss practical ways to cultivate spiritual awarenessвҖ”both individually and then with other people. The beauty is that whether you consider yourself spiritual or not, this episode will open your eyes and unlock an entirely new way of looking at life. title: The Metrics: Blood Sugar Basics!
summary: In this gentle and empowering episode, CatherineвҖ”host of The Diabetic MammyвҖ”welcomes both new and seasoned parents of children with type 1 diabetes. Titled вҖңThe Metrics,вҖқ this episode is inspired by two mothers who, despite their different points on the diabetes journey, both revealed they had never been clearly taught what key diabetes numbers mean. This episode walks listeners through the essential metrics of diabetes management with warmth and clarity: Glycemic Control вҖ“ the overall aim of managing blood sugars in a safe range, with emphasis on balance over perfection.Blood Glucose Levels (BGL) вҖ“ explained in both mmol/L and mg/dL, with realistic goals tailored to each child.HbA1c вҖ“ a long-term measure of average blood sugars, usually targeted under 7%, but personalized.Time in Range (TIR) вҖ“ a newer CGM-based concept reflecting how often blood sugars stay in the target range. Catherine reassures listeners that understanding these numbers is a process and that it's okay not to have it all figured out at once. She emphasizes that good glycemic control supports physical health, energy, and emotional well-being, but also reminds parents that numbers arenвҖҷt everythingвҖ”their childвҖҷs joy, safety, and spirit matter just as much. She closes with an uplifting message of community, validation, and encouragement: You are doing a great job. YouвҖҷre not alone. And this isnвҖҷt a raceвҖ”itвҖҷs a relationship. The Diabetic Mammy is the heartfelt, no-nonsense podcast where type 1 diabetes meets motherhood, community, and a whole lot of real talk. Hosted by Catherine, a mammy navigating the highs and lows of type 1 diabetes, each episode blends personal insight with practical wisdom, offering support for parents, carers, and anyone living with or loving someone with diabetes. From tech transitions and hospital diagnoses to school lunches and stolen sleep, this podcast embraces the messy, meaningful moments that make up life with chronic illness. Whether youвҖҷre newly diagnosed or a seasoned warrior, The Diabetic Mammy is your space to feel seen, supported, and never alone. Connect with Catherine by email on catherine@abundantlydiabetes.com or reach her on Instagram: abundantlydiabetes  Hosted on Acast. See acast.com/privacy for more information.

summary: Curious to know your biological age and what it might tell you about your health? In this episode - brought to you by GlycanAge - Liz and Nikolina take a deep dive into the fascinating science of glycans, explain the differences between your chronological and biological age, and look at different factors that may affect the speed of ageing, such as stress, hormones, and exercise. title: How to Hold On to What Really Matters, with Jo Malone
summary: Essential Habits is a new weekly series from High Performance. Hosted by Damian Hughes, each episode breaks down powerful habits from world-class performers and thinkers - so you can apply them in your own life. HereвҖҷs DamianвҖҷs take on the first episode, featuring Jo Malone:  "Sometimes life moves so fast we forget we even can stop. ThatвҖҷs something Jo reminded me of in this episode, the quiet power of pausing and taking stock before charging ahead. JoвҖҷs story is a masterclass in resilience and reinvention. With around 6,000 thoughts flying through our minds each day, many of them negative, itвҖҷs not just about thinking less, but choosing whatвҖҷs worth holding on to. Together, we explore how stopping to organise our thoughts and truly understand our story can turn moments of crisis into turning points. I also unpack a fascinating piece of neuroscience, a study with a less-than-glamorous title, but a powerful message, showing how our brains naturally prioritise rewarding memories. If youвҖҷve ever wanted to hold on to what matters and let the rest slip awayвҖҰ this oneвҖҷs for you." Here is a list of studies referenced in the episode: The Persistence and Transience of Memory. Authors: Blake A. Richards & Paul W. FranklandEbbinghaus's Forgetting Curve. Author: Hermann EbbinghausDiscovery of 'Thought WormsвҖҷ. Researchers: QueenвҖҷs University, CanadaEyewitness Misidentification and Wrongful Convictions. Organisation: Innocence ProjectPost-learning Hippocampal Dynamics Promote Preferential Retention of Rewarding Events. Authors: Matthias J. Gruber, Maureen Ritchey, Shao-Fang Wang, Manoj K. Doss, Charan RanganathWhy We Forget and How to Remember Better. Authors: Andrew E. Budson & Elizabeth A. Kensinger

WeвҖҷre joined by Ronit Menashe and Vida Delrahim, the co-founders of WeNatal, a doctor-backed prenatal supplement brand thatвҖҷs putting male fertility and sperm health where it belongs: at the center of the conversation. summary: Save 20% on all Nuzest Products WORLDWIDE with the code MIKKIPEDIA at www.nuzest.co.nz, www.nuzest.com.au or www.nuzest.com This week on the podcast, Mikki speaks to Marco AltiniвҖ”data scientist, athlete, and founder of HRV4TrainingвҖ”about his personal and professional exploration into periodised nutrition. Known for his evidence-based insights into heart rate variability and training readiness, Marco has recently turned the lens inward, experimenting with how varying his fuelling strategies around training intensity and adaptation goals can impact performance and recovery. Marco holds a PhD in applied machine learning, a M.Sc.В  in computer science engineering, and a M.Sc. n human movement sciences and high-performance coaching. His work has been published in peer-reviewed journals, and heвҖҷs a frequent voice in the endurance and sports science communities. Through his own training and experimentation, Marco explores the integration of physiological dataвҖ”like HRV, resting heart rate, and glucose monitoringвҖ”with periodised nutrition and recovery strategies. title: How to Advocate for Your Health in 6 Minutes: Functional Health Coach Debbie Wright on Bloodwork, Wearables, and Doctor Visits
summary: This is StRest! In this episode, Kerstin Lindquist is joined by her best friend and board-certified functional health coach, Debbie Wright, for an empowering, practical conversation that will change how you think about your health. From how to prepare for your doctorвҖҷs visit to what wearables actually matter, they walk through the top five things you can do to get the most out of your (often brief) medical appointments. Kerstin and Debbie unpack the truth behind вҖңnormalвҖқ bloodwork ranges, how to interpret your health data from devices like WHOOP and UltraHuman, and why glucose monitoring may be the single most powerful thing you can do to prevent chronic disease. They also model exactly what to say to your doctor when they push back, how to self-advocate with grace, and what functional medicine can offer when traditional healthcare falls short. If youвҖҷve ever walked out of a doctorвҖҷs appointment frustrated or confused, this episode gives you a roadmapвҖ”and the mindsetвҖ”to walk in prepared, collaborative, and confident. Major Themes & Concepts вң… What your wearable metrics (HRV, RHR, VO2 Max, etc.) actually mean вң… How to talk to your doctorвҖ”even if you only have six minutes вң… Why вҖңnormalвҖқ lab ranges arenвҖҷt the goal (and what to ask for instead) вң… Connection between glucose spikes, sleep, stress, and chronic disease вң… How food scanning apps help you beat marketing lies on food labels вң… Power of informed consent and requesting blood panels with confidence вң… Prevention is cheaper than sick care вң… What to do when insurance wonвҖҷt help вң… How knowing your genetic risks can make you healthier, not more afraid вң… Functional medicine vs. traditional care: building your full health team вң… Why you deserve collaborative careвҖ”and how to find the right provider Chapter Breakdown 00:00 вҖ“ Introduction to Health and Wellness Coaching 05:12 вҖ“ Maximizing Your Doctor's Appointment 08:01 вҖ“ The Power of Wearable Technology 10:38 вҖ“ Understanding Health Metrics (HRV, VO2 Max, Deep Sleep, etc.) 13:38 вҖ“ Proactive Health Management and Using Data to Drive Change 16:13 вҖ“ Navigating Blood Work and Doctor Relationships 28:21 вҖ“ Understanding Health Metrics and Blood Work (continued) 32:10 вҖ“ The Importance of Glucose Monitoring 34:19 вҖ“ Navigating Doctor Consultations and Medication Prescriptions 36:24 вҖ“ Food Scanning and Label Reading for Health Awareness 40:13 вҖ“ Genetics, Lifestyle, and Beating the Odds 42:33 вҖ“ Finding the Right Healthcare Provider for You 45:52 вҖ“ Empowering Yourself in Doctor Visits and Knowing What to Say 50:51 вҖ“ Building a Supportive Health Community and Advocating for Yourself 58:15 вҖ“ CTA: Join Coaching, Get the Blood Panel List, Plug Into the Community Resources & Recommendations Work with Kerstin рҹ“… Workshops & Events вҖ“ Join upcoming events & retreats. рҹ’Ў Health Coaching with Kerstin вҖ“ 1:1 coaching for stress reduction & wellness. title: Wellness Unmasked: Exploring Autoimmune Diseases with Dr. Alexander Torres
podcast_name: Wellness Unmasked with Dr. Nicole Saphier
summary: In the inaugural episode of Wellness Unmasked, Dr. Nicole Saphier shares her personal journey from being a doctor to becoming a patient. She discusses her struggles with autoimmune disease, the importance of finding the right healthcare provider, and the integration of both Western and Eastern medicine in her treatment plan. Dr. Saphier is joined by Dr. Alexander Torres, a rheumatologist, they discuss the complexities of autoimmune diseases, their rising incidence, and the impact of diet, environment, and lifestyle on health. Dr. Torres emphasizes the importance of understanding the immune system, the role of COVID-19, and the hygiene hypothesis in relation to autoimmune conditions. Wellness Unmasked with Dr. Nicole Saphier is part of the Clay Travis & Buck Sexton Podcast Network - new episodes debut every Tuesday & Friday.В  See omnystudio.com/listener for privacy information.

title: Fierce Self Compassion: How We Harness Kindness to Speak Up and Thrive. With Kristin Neff.
summary: Welcome to another favorite encore edition of Untangle.  Kristin Neff is an extremely well-regarded expert and pioneer in the field of self-compassion. Her most recent book is called Fierce Self Compassion: How Women can Harness Kindness to Speak Up, Claim their Power and Thrive. She discusses how self-compassion allows us to be tender with ourselves in difficult situations, but also that it sometimes needs to be fierce вҖ” like the mama bear energy that comes from a place of love, service, and a desire to reduce harm. We must, she says, act courageously to protect ourselves from harm and injustice вҖ” and to motivate necessary change in ourselves and society. Her messageвҖҰwomen can be fierce and tender. Best quote from the book: "Tender Self-Compassion harnesses the energy of nurturing to alleviate suffering while fierce Self-Compassion harnesses the energy of action to alleviate suffering." We need both. As always, weвҖҷre Untangle, the podcast from Muse, the brain sensing headband dedicated to unlocking your brains potential.В  WeвҖҷre here, just like youвҖҰto learn, grow, share and connect. We hope you enjoy this episode. Learn more about your ad choices. Visit megaphone.fm/adchoices

title: Is вҖҳare you ok?вҖҷ a good question and how your eyes give away memory precision
summary: Are you ok?   ItвҖҷs a question that might be at the front of your mind during a traumatic event. But how helpful is it and can it even make matters worse? We answer a very intriguing listener question.   And in the studio with Claudia is Catherine Loveday, Professor of Cognitive Neuroscience, whoвҖҷs excited about a study that has uncovered a fascinating way of measuring just how precise our memory is. Clue: ItвҖҷs all about the eyes.  We also reveal the latest finalist in the All in the Mind awards. This week it's Dr Rajeev Kumar who was nominated by Holly Batyka-Berry for his support through Holly's experiences of severe postnatal depression.    The awards take place at the BBC Radio Theatre in London on 18 June and there is a ballot for free tickets which closes at midday on 31 May. You can enter online by going to www.bbc.co.uk/showsandtours/shows.   If youвҖҷd like information on sources of support for post-natal depression do go to bbc.co.uk/actionline    Presenter: Claudia Hammond  Producer: Gerry Holt and Hannah Fisher  Studio Manager: Bob Nettles  Production Coordintator: Siobhan Maguire  Content Editor: Glyn Tansley

summary: In this episode John Oliver speaks to expert guests about evaluating the suitability of unlicensed medicines for the patient. title: Fertility Action Network- A new charity for Fertility Support

summary: We have got an extra episode on the podcast this week as we are celebrating the launch of the new fertility support charity- Fertility Action Network. We discuss Katie's personal experience with infertility and IVF.How The Fertility Action Network aims to improve access to fertility treatments.Why there is a significant disparity in fertility treatment access based on location.Why mental health support is crucial for those undergoing fertility treatments.How male fertility issues are often overlooked in the fertility conversation.How education and resources for patients are lacking in the current system.The importance of community support for those struggling with fertility.How the campaign seeks to address inequalities in fertility treatment access.How Therapy played a vital role in Katie's recovery and advocacy work.Why engaging with MPs and raising awareness is essential for change. title: Гүpisode 88 : Le syndrome des ovaires polykystiques (SOPK)
summary: AujourdвҖҷhui, nous parlons dвҖҷun sujet qui touche des millions de femmes dans le monde, souvent sans mГӘme quвҖҷelles ne le sachent : le syndrome des ovaires polykystiques, plus connu sous le nom de SOPK. En particulier nous aborderons son lien avec la rГ©sistance Г  lвҖҷinsuline et ce quвҖҷil est possible de faire pour lвҖҷamГ©liorer ou le renverser.В  Statistiques sur le SOPK  Les critГЁres diagnostiques :  Anovulation chronique ou ovulation irrГ©guliГЁre HyperandrogГ©nie clinique ou biologique Ovaires polykystiques Г  lвҖҷГ©chographie Ce ne sont pas des kystes.  ProblГЁme de fertilitГ©, mais aussi dГ©sordre mГ©tabolique et hormonal complexe qui affecte la santГ© physique, mentale et Г©motionnelle des femmes qui en sont atteintes. Les phГ©notypes  Le SOPK ne se limite pas aux ovaires, et est Г©volutif.  La rГ©sistance Г  lвҖҷinsuline est lвҖҷun des mГ©canismes les plus courants associГ©s au SOPK, mais elle nвҖҷest pas seule en cause.  Les symptГҙmes courants du SOPK LвҖҷinsuline, la pierre angulaireВ  Le test de KraftPourquoi toutes les femmes qui ont une rГ©sistance Г  l'insuline n'ont pas toutes un SOPK?  Les approches thГ©rapeutiques naturelles et pharmacologiques Le jeГ»ne et le cycle menstruel  Les produits naturels qui peuvent aider  LвҖҷapproche pharmacologique : module les symptГҙmes CвҖҷest maintenant lвҖҷheure de lвҖҷastuce de la semaine :В il faut Г©couter l'Г©pisode pour la connaГ®tre  Les messages clГ©s de lвҖҷГ©pisode sont :В  Le SOPK est un syndrome systГ©mique, pas seulement un trouble ovarien.Il affecte plusieurs systГЁmes du corps (mГ©tabolique, hormonal, Г©motionnel) et ne se limite pas Г  la fertilitГ© ni Г  la prГ©sence de kystes sur les ovaires. La rГ©sistance Г  lвҖҷinsuline est un facteur central du SOPK.Elle concerne jusquвҖҷГ  70 % des femmes atteintes, parfois de faГ§on subtile, et alimente un cercle vicieux hormonal et inflammatoire aux consГ©quences multiples. Une prise en charge globale et personnalisГ©e est essentielle.Nutrition adaptГ©e, exercice musculaire, gestion du stress, bon sommeil et, au besoin, soutien pharmacologique ou naturel permettent dвҖҷagir sur les causes profondes plutГҙt que de masquer les symptГҙmes. Nous aimerions remercier chaleureusement la gynГ©cologue Martine Aubry pour sa relecture du prГ©sent Г©pisode et ses conseils judicieux.В  Pour en savoir plus sur le SOPK et son lien avec la rГ©sistance Г  lвҖҷinsuline et que vous lisez lвҖҷanglais, nous vous suggГ©rons le livre PCOS Plan de Nadia Pateguana, prГ©facГ© par Dr Jason Fung.В  www.cuisinemed.com HГ©bergГ© par Ausha. Visitez ausha.co/politique-de-confidentialite pour plus d'informations.

title: The Truth About Your Hormones
summary: Nobody warned us that perimenopause could start at 35. That the brain fog, panic attacks, itchy skin, heavy periods, or sudden weight gain might be your hormones, not stress, not ageing, not something to just push through. Our mothers didnвҖҷt talk about it. Not because they didnвҖҷt care, but because they didnвҖҷt have the words either.В  We were told menopause happens at 50. No one mentioned the decade before it. This podcast changes that.В  It gives you language for what you're going through, stories that make you feel less alone, and the truth about perimenopause and menopause that no one ever explained. Because feeling lost, angry, or unlike yourself isnвҖҷt weakness. ItвҖҷs a signal, and you shouldnвҖҷt have to figure it out alone.

title: How to have a difficult conversation
summary: Welcome to WhatвҖҷs Up Docs?, the podcast where doctors and identical twins Chris and Xand van Tulleken explore every aspect of our health and wellbeing.    How do you tell your friend that something theyвҖҷve been doing is really starting to hurt your feelings? Talk to your mum about her health? Or even ask your partner to try something new in bed? If just thinking about these types of conversations makes your stomach flip, youвҖҷre not alone. Most of us avoid moments like these, but how important is it to confront our interpersonal issues head on?  The docs are joined by Chartered Psychologist Kimberley Wilson to explore how we can better prepare for lifeвҖҷs tough but important conversations. Chris and Xand are committed to sharing their personal health and wellbeing journey on WhatвҖҷs up Docs, and in this episode they unexpectedly found themselves having a difficult conversation of their own. If youвҖҷd like to share your thoughts on difficult conversations or anything else that caught your attention in this episode - you can email us at whatsupdocs@bbc.co.uk or Whatsapp us on 08000 665123. title: Welcome to The Diabetic Mammy
summary: Warm, candid, empowering pilot episode, in which Catherine introduces The Diabetic MammyвҖ”a podcast dedicated to navigating the emotional, practical, and social complexities of living with type 1 diabetes, especially in the context of motherhood. Sharing her own journey from being diagnosed at age 4 to becoming a mother of two daughters (both also diagnosed with type 1 diabetes), Catherine invites listeners into a space of honesty, humour, and community. She explains her motivation for launching the podcast: to create a supportive environment for diabetic mammies who often juggle invisible challenges like burnout, mom guilt, and balancing self-care with parenting. Catherine emphasises that this podcast is not medical advice but a place of real talk, real stories, and shared experiences. Please connect with Catherine on Instagram @ abundantlydiabetes or email her  The Diabetic Mammy is the heartfelt, no-nonsense podcast where type 1 diabetes meets motherhood, community, and a whole lot of real talk. Hosted by Catherine, a mammy navigating the highs and lows of type 1 diabetes, each episode blends personal insight with practical wisdom, offering support for parents, carers, and anyone living with or loving someone with diabetes. From tech transitions and hospital diagnoses to school lunches and stolen sleep, this podcast embraces the messy, meaningful moments that make up life with chronic illness. Whether youвҖҷre newly diagnosed or a seasoned warrior, The Diabetic Mammy is your space to feel seen, supported, and never alone. Connect with Catherine by email on catherine@abundantlydiabetes.com or reach her on Instagram: abundantlydiabetes  Hosted on Acast. See acast.com/privacy for more information.

title: 4: How to prepare for an Expedition - Dr Andrew Graham Part 2
summary: Today we sit down with Dr Andrew Graham. Andrew has worked as orthopedic surgeon and wilderness medic for the past 35 years. He has supported and completed numerous endurance events and adventure races in Australia, Morocco, Borneo, Fiji and New Zealand. Most notably he has provided medical support and completed expeditions across the infamous Kokoda Trail in Papua New Guinea over 15 times.

title: 3: Medicine on the high seas with Dr Shannon Mulder
summary: Dr Shannon Mulder is a senior registrar in emergency medicine who began her career in South Africa, before transitioning to a career providing care on cruise ships. Her career has taken her to almost every continent in the world. ShannonВ now calls Australia home, and works as a retrievalist for the Royal Flying Doctor Service in the Northern Territory.

title: 2: Endurance in the Jungles with Dr Andrew Graham - Part 1
summary: Today we sit down with Dr Andrew Graham. Andrew has worked as orthopedic surgeon and wilderness medic for the past 35 years. He has supported and completed endurance events and adventure races in Australia, Morocco, Borneo, Fiji and New Zealand. He has also provided medical support and completed expeditions across the infamous Kokoda Trail in Papua New Guinea over 15 times.

title: 1: Jellyfish and remote medicine in the far north of Australia. - Mr Mark Ward
summary: Today we sit down with Mr Mark Ward. Mark is a commercial skipper, avid fisherman and paramedic from Weipa in Queensland, Australia. We talk about life in the north, the challenges of delivering medicine in remote environments and what it's like to be stung by Australia's deadliest marine creature, the Box jellyfish.
